segments.text: Jamaica urgently to speak to our CNN reporter there, Derek Van Damme, who's one of CNN's meteorologists. Melissa, we'll come back to you in a second, but Derek, I don't know how well you can hear me. You can see the conditions from how you're standing. Can you tell us the latest from where you are? Peter, yeah, listen, we decided to venture to the coastline of Jamaica. We were in more of a sheltered location earlier today.

Since the storm has passed this location to our north, we wanted to see how the capital city of Jamaica fared. Look, we're getting absolutely melted with very strong winds and very intense rain, but I'm happy to report that the city was spared the worst of the catastrophic damage. We have driven around the city. We have seen some electrical poles that were tilted to the side, a couple of field boards that had fallen over, some minor

Flooding on the roadways, but by and by the storm spared this city the population of a million. But I want to reiterate that we're not out of the woods first and foremost, clearly. And what is happening and unfolding over the western parts of the country as what is now a category four hurricane rips through western Jamaica, about to exit off the north coast. Think about all the tourism, Montego Bay, the International Airport.

The parishes of Westmoreland and St. Elizabeth, these parishes, there's 14 of them across Jamaica. The western parts of the country are the breadbasket of this country, so there's no doubt that agriculture has been impacted. The economy will be tough to recover from this hurricane, not to mention every once in a while. Sorry, I get interrupted because.

Wind will take my breath away for a second. Look, bottom line is Kingston was spared, but we're not out of the woods. But we are heartbreaks for what is happening to our west. I mean, if we're this far away from the center of the storm, 150 kilometers, it's an idea of just how intense it is where the inner core of the eye wall made landfall and where it continues to move. Yeah, Derek Van Dyke, the latest on Hurricane Melissa that has made landfall in Jamaica.
Not just telling us about it, but also giving us a very visual reminder of the conditions facing Jamaicans there, and we'll continue to update you on the situation there.

segments.text: For the lives of women have been more affected by its new horizons than those of any other group. Profound and stirring as have been accomplishments in the remoter fields of pure research, it is in the home that the applications of scientific achievement have perhaps been most far reaching. And it is through changing conditions there that women have become the greatest beneficiaries in the modern scheme. Science has released them from much.

segments.text: And if you would ask me this afternoon to speak my mind, that I take to be not all the flattering things that you would like to hear, but some of those other things that cause such an one as myself some concern. Before I get on to talking about that, allow me to tell you a little bit about myself so that you can see my background, my slants, and my biases to know, as the American phrase is, and I've learned some of it.
